etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "speech") + phonetic 夸(夸) (kuā, "boast") -> boastful speech
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 言(言) (yán, "speech") for its semantic value, suggesting something spoken or related to words, with 夸(夸) (kuā, "boast") providing the pronunciation. The result is a word associated with boastful or exaggerated speech.
etymology.simplification: The traditional 言(言) (yán, "speech") was removed, leaving only the component 夸(夸) (kuā, "boast") as the simplified character.

examples.chinese: 他总是喜欢夸自己的儿子(他總是喜歡誇自己的兒子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì xǐhuān kuā zìjǐ de érzi.
examples.english: He always likes to boast about his son.

examples.chinese: 她夸奖自己在比赛中的表现(她誇獎自己在比賽中的表現)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kuājiǎng zìjǐ zài bǐsài zhōng de biǎoxiàn.
examples.english: She boasted about her performance in the competition.

examples.chinese: 老师夸赞他的进步很大(老師誇讚他的進步很大)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī kuāzàn tā de jìnbù hěn dà.
examples.english: The teacher boasted about his great progress.
